channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Ever miss the classic HBO channels? Well, Max is now testing a similar experience that solves the conundrum of what to watch
item.description: Max is taking a page from Peacock and Disney Plus and rolling out a live-feed of shows and movies dubbed channels.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: Meta to build $10 billion AI data center in Louisiana as Elon Musk expands his Tennessee AI facility
item.description: Facebook’s parent company Meta says it will build a $10 billion artificial intelligence data center in northeast Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Jack Brook And Adrian Sainz, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: District of Columbia says Amazon secretly stopped fast deliveries to 2 predominantly Black ZIP codes
item.description: The District of Columbia sued Amazon on Wednesday, alleging the company secretly stopped providing its fastest delivery service to residents of two predominantly Black neighborhoods while still charging millions of dollars for a membership that promises the benefit.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump taps Gail Slater to lead DOJ antitrust division
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday tapped Gail Slater, a former adviser to Vice President-elect JD Vance, to lead the Justice Department’s antitrust division.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Sam Altman brushes off Musk's influence in Trump administration amid tension
item.description: Sam Altman, the CEO of tech company OpenAI, brushed off tech billionaire Elon Musk's influence in the upcoming Trump administration, according to a Wednesday report from The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: Kyndryl, Nokia expand AI, quantum-safe connectivity options
item.description: Kyndryl is extending its relationship with Nokia and expanding its menu of integration services by including more of Nokia’s data center networking products.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nomination of Paul Atkins as SEC Chair Raises Concerns for Crypto Industry
item.description: Paul Atkins nomination as SEC chair raises concerns for crypto industry, regulatory scrutiny expected.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Hands on with Microsoft’s Windows Recall: Not impressive yet
item.description: When Microsoft announced Recall earlier this year, the move took the tech world by storm — and not in a good way. Recall is supposed to help you find almost anything you see on your PC by taking screenshots in the background and turning your everyday Windows usage into a searchable database.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Cython tutorial: How to speed up Python
item.description: Python is a powerful programming language that is easy to learn and easy to work with, but it is not always the fastest to run—especially when you’re dealing with math or statistics.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stanford misinformation expert admits his chatbot use led to misinformation in sworn federal court filing
item.description: The professor's legal imbroglio illustrates one of the most common problems with an AI technology that has taken the world by storm.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025
item.description: Fiscal 2025 is shaping up to be busy for the Army National Guard as it pushes to complete migration of all end-user devices to Entra ID and Intune. The post Army Guard to prioritize application visibility across states and territories in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: TikTok defends handling of Romania election content in grilling by EU lawmakers
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — TikTok took down several networks that tried to meddle in Romania's elections, executives said Tuesday as they defended the company's election integrity measures to European Union lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN and STEPHEN McGRATH, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 2025 will be a bad year for remote work
item.description: “Mr. Musk Goes to Washington.” And he’s planning to roll back remote work in the federal government. Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk and one-time presidential candidate Vivek Ramaswamy are expected to be appointed by incoming president Donald J.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Two years of ChatGPT: the conversation that never ends
item.description: Two years can be an eternity for the technology industry: plenty of time for enterprises to innovate, launch a new product, peak on the stock market… and then plummet again. Think about the bubbles that briefly surrounded 3D printing, smart glasses, or the metaverse.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Siberia's 'Gateway to Hell' crater fuels new fears
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says the Batagaika crater in Siberia expands 35 million cubic feet yearly due to thawing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Australian Parliament bans social media for under-16s with world-first law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — A social media ban for children under 16 passed the Australian Parliament on Friday in a world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canada sues Google over alleged anticompetitive practices in online ads
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canada's antitrust watchdog said Thursday it is suing Google over alleged anticompetitive conduct in the tech giant’s online advertising business and wants the company to sell off two of its ad tech services and pay a penalty.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cloud providers turn to custom chips to meet high demand
item.description: Squeezed between AI-driven demand for ever-faster computing, and shortages of the GPUs used to accelerate those workloads, hyperscalers are designing custom silicon for specific workloads to further improve performance while cutting costs.
item.dc:creator: Network World
